description: Test vectors for Anthropic API request signing validation. These vectors provide reference implementations for Cloud KMS request signing using ECDSA P-256 and RSA-PSS algorithms.

test_vectors.description: ECDSA P-256 with SHA-256 signature validation

keys.private_key_pem:
-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
MHcCAQEEIAfKFGEXiZvfkF11qSYL34BUH7OO4Db42sdkFW8oxRzpoAoGCCqGSM49
AwEHoUQDQgAEzOj+vTSESLowQf8FY84ZlRkQ2hDQ4UsDUfUvEfU9QC/g9bYiXOMl
AyDDKCSAOhbzmZNF7BcnaEV3Nfppg3Ytmg==
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----

test_vectors.notes: ECDSA signatures using RFC 6979 are deterministic - the same input and key will always produce the same signature. Implementations using RFC 6979 should match this signature exactly. Non-RFC 6979 implementations will produce different (but still valid) signatures.

test_vectors.notes: RSA-PSS signatures are NON-DETERMINISTIC due to random salt. Each signature will be different, but all valid signatures will verify correctly with the public key. Do not compare signature bytes directly - only verify that your signature validates successfully.

implementation_notes.signature_validation: Implementations should verify that their generated signatures validate successfully with the provided public keys. Do not compare exact signature bytes - focus on validation.
implementation_notes.timestamp: The 'created' timestamp (1762970351) in test vectors is set to November 5, 2025. When making real API requests, use current Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). Signatures expire 5 minutes after creation.

determinism.ecdsa: Test vector uses RFC 6979 deterministic ECDSA. Implementations using RFC 6979 will produce this exact signature. Implementations using random nonces will produce different (but valid) signatures. Both approaches are cryptographically secure.
determinism.rsa_pss: RSA-PSS signatures are always non-deterministic due to random salt per RFC 8017. Each signature will differ, even with identical inputs.
